title: Spoon Carving & Axe Workshop
description: Learn the ancient art of green woodworking. Master safe axe and knife techniques, understand wood grain, and design your own beautiful hand-carved cherry wood wooden cooking spoon.
longDescription: Green woodworking is a craft centered on working with fresh, unseasoned timber. This workshop guides you through the properties of split wood, tracing grain directions, and using Swedish sloyd knives alongside lightweight carving axes. You will start with a raw birch or cherry limb, learn splitting and hewing, and end with a beautifully burnished spoon of your own design. Ideal for both absolute beginners looking for hand-tool confidence and advanced woodworkers looking for relaxed craft meditation.
instructorBio: Artisan green woodworker specializing in Swedish axe and knife spoon-carving techniques.

title: Precision Tool Sharpening & Care
description: A sharp woodworker is a happy woodworker. Bring your own hand planes, chisels, or carving knives. Learn Japanese waterstone sharpening, leather stropping, and plane iron adjustment.
longDescription: Tired of tearing grain and struggling through joints? This interactive workshop teaches you how to establish correct micro-bevel angles on waterstones, hone chisels till they easily shave paper or hair, and tune traditional hand planes to shave down-grain tissue-thin shavings of oak. You will also learn rust prevention, tool selection, and custom stropping board creation to keep your shop accessories in immaculate condition.
instructorBio: Fine cabinetry restoration expert with a passion for Japanese waterstones and water-resistant oils.

title: Introduction to Hand-Cut Joinery
description: Discover the strength and beauty of traditional jointing methods. Spend a day diving into layout layout tools, sawing fine lines, chopping timber, and building a secure housing joint and bridle joint.
longDescription: Traditional carpentry joins wood without screws, nails, or modern metal fasteners, leveraging geometric lockouts and hide glues. In this masterclass, you will learn the precise geometry of mortise and tenon joinery, bridle joints, and sliding dovetails. Step-by-step layout with marking gauges and cutting with Japanese pull saws will give you hand skills to make heavy structural frames or delicate frames that will last generations.
instructorBio: Historic joinery master. Author of traditional English structural timber-framing blueprints.

title: Rustic Dovetail Keepsafe Box
description: Craft an elegant heirloom container with through-dovetails from local English Oak and Walnut. Master chiseling wood drawers and fitting brass hinges with millimeter precision.
longDescription: The dovetail joint is widely regarded as the ultimate hallmark of artisan craftsmanship. Over two packed days, we will layout and cut a custom-fitted key-and-keepsake box. You will learn to pair a striking dark Walnut lid with a local English Oak body, hand-saw tight pin-and-tail boards, chisel away small socket waste, and fit fine inset hinges. You will leave with a polished, luxury keepsake token to show on your bedside.
instructorBio: Artisan green woodworker specializing in Swedish axe and knife spoon-carving techniques.

title: Introduction to Woodturning
description: Mount solid hardwoods on a rotating lathe and chisel out elegant, symmetrical profiles like bowls, rolling pins, and dynamic table legs.
longDescription: Woodturning is one of the most immediate and thrilling woodworking disciplines. Watch raw blocks of Ash and Sycamore instantly reform into dynamic bowls, spindles, or handles right under your chisels. Learn correct headstock mounting, spindle gouge angle angles, parting tools, and safe sanding techniques. You will finish by making a gorgeous, custom wooden honey-dipper and kitchen bowl.
instructorBio: Fine cabinetry restoration expert with a passion for Japanese waterstones and water-resistant oils.

title: Sugi & Cypress Japanese Joinery Masterclass
description: Unlock complex sliding interlocks like Kanawa Tsugi. We work with premium Cypress woods, hand planes (Kanna), and authentic paring chisels.
longDescription: In this masterclass, explore the meditative discipline of Japanese sashimono joinery. We study wood expansion patterns and construct traditional scarf and splice joints that interlock perfectly without a single drop of glue. Work with cedar, sugi wood, and master pulling tools rather than pushing tools, achieving pristine geometric joints.
instructorBio: Historic joinery master. Author of traditional English structural timber-framing blueprints.
